content: We have an open question about feed-forward vs per-scene reconstruction. What does the corpus say?

reasoning: Read the existing question doc, then enumerate the LRM-family papers, then use the ask tool for a synthesis pass.
content: Let me read the open-question doc first, then enumerate the feed-forward family, then run a synthesis question through `ask`.

content.body: Feed-forward reconstruction vs per-scene optimisation: where does each win?... Until a head-to-head evaluation suite reports (a) reconstruction fidelity on held-out views, (b) geometric error vs ground truth, (c) wall-clock inference, (d) GPU-hour amortised cost per asset, both bets can be defended without evidence.

nodes.name: LRM: Large Reconstruction Model for Single Image to 3D

content.title: LRM: Large Reconstruction Model for Single Image to 3D
content.body: Hong et al. train a 500M-parameter Transformer to map an image to a triplane radiance field in ~5 seconds. Trained end-to-end on Objaverse + MVImgNet, ~1M objects.

content.title: LGM: Large Multi-View Gaussian Model for High-Resolution 3D Content Creation
content.body: Tang et al. swap LRM's triplane for a multi-view Gaussian decoder; takes a 4-view multi-view-diffusion output and emits a dense Gaussian field at high resolution in ~5 seconds.

content.title: TripoSR: Fast 3D Object Reconstruction from a Single Image
content.body: Tochilkin et al. open-source a production-quality LRM-style reconstructor: improved data filtering on Objaverse, retrained at 1.5x parameters, ~0.5 second inference on an A100. Output is a triplane NeRF with mesh export.

arguments.question: On the same input image, how does feed-forward 3D reconstruction (LRM/LGM/TripoSR) compare to per-scene SDS-on-splats (DreamGaussian) in fidelity, geometric accuracy, and wall-clock cost?

content.answer: The corpus does not contain a single head-to-head benchmark across feed-forward and per-scene SDS-on-splats methods on a shared input set, but five self-reports triangulate the trade-off. Feed-forward methods (LRM ~5s/object, LGM ~5s, TripoSR ~0.5s on A100) produce coherent geometry but blur high-frequency detail because the triplane / Gaussian decoder is bandwidth-limited and trained against Objaverse renders that under-represent thin structures. Per-scene SDS-on-splats methods (DreamGaussian ~2 minutes, AGG ~30s as an amortised variant) produce sharper appearance but occasionally hallucinate broken or doubled geometry, especially on the back side of the object where the SDS critic has weak supervision. None of the five papers reports geometric error against the same ground-truth meshes, and none reports a fixed-input PSNR comparison. The economics flip at asset volume: LGM/TripoSR amortise training cost at production-scale (>10k assets); DreamGaussian-style SDS wins below that threshold.

sources.snippet: LRM trains a 500M-parameter Transformer on Objaverse to produce a triplane in ~5 seconds.
LGM takes a 4-view diffusion output and emits a Gaussian field at high resolution.
TripoSR retrains LRM with improved data filtering; ~0.5s inference on an A100.
DreamGaussian swaps the SDS backbone for 3D Gaussians; per-asset cost ~2 minutes.
AGG amortises single-image Gaussian generation; intermediate between feed-forward and per-scene.

reasoning: No lint report yet. Move on. The answer plus the existing question doc is enough to propose an experiment design.
content:
Lint report doesn't exist yet for this build. Here is what I have:

**What the corpus already says**
- The open question doc (`questions/inference-cost-vs-fidelity.md`) calls out the four-axis gap: fidelity / geometric accuracy / wall-clock / GPU-hour amortised cost. No paper provides all four.
- The feed-forward family is LRM (arxiv-2311-04400) -> Instant3D (arxiv-2311-06214) -> LGM (arxiv-2402-05054) -> TripoSR (arxiv-2403-02151). All three I read fit in ~0.5-5s/asset.
- The per-scene SDS-on-splats family is DreamGaussian (arxiv-2309-16653) and AGG (arxiv-2401-04099, which is itself an amortised hybrid). DreamGaussian is ~2 minutes/asset.

**New evidence the `ask` synthesis pulled together**
- Feed-forward methods are bandwidth-limited on thin structures because Objaverse under-represents them.
- Per-scene SDS hallucinates back-side geometry where the critic has weak supervision.
- The break-even at >10k assets favours feed-forward; below that, DreamGaussian wins.

**Proposed experiment to resolve the question** (extending the existing doc):
  Inputs:  100 single images sampled stratified across (synthetic / GSO / in-the-wild) and (thin / blocky / organic) categories.
  Methods: LRM, LGM, TripoSR (feed-forward) and DreamGaussian, AGG (per-scene/amortised).
  Metrics:
    (a) Held-out-view PSNR / SSIM / LPIPS on the 50 GSO captures (ground truth available).
    (b) Chamfer + F-score against GSO meshes.
    (c) Wall-clock inference on a single A100, single asset.
    (d) Amortised GPU-hours per asset across 100, 1k, 10k, 100k asset volumes.
  Stratify (a)+(b) by category to expose the thin-structure failure mode the synthesis predicts.

If any of {a, b, c, d} is missing the comparison can be made (this corpus has roughly enough data to estimate three out of four). I'd extend the open-question doc with this design rather than rewrite it.
